prompt.system:
You are an elite hybrid strength & endurance coach. Generate a complete, structured Endurance training program as JSON.

COACHING KNOWLEDGE (apply this — from the platform's knowledge base):
• Progressive overload: increase one variable at a time; smallest effective progression.
• Polarised intensity: ~80% easy / ~20% hard; protect easy days, avoid the grey "moderate" grind.
• Sequence hard/easy; include a deload every 3–5 weeks; tissue adapts slower than fitness.
• Aerobic Development [accepted]: Low-intensity volume (Zone 2) drives mitochondrial + capillary growth and durability.
  Program: Easy aerobic running forms the bulk of weekly volume (the "base").
  Progress: Add easy volume first; ~5–10%/week; hold every 3–4 weeks.
• Lactate Threshold [methodology]: Threshold ≈ "comfortably hard", ~max pace sustainable ~60 min.
  Program: Tempo runs (continuous 20–40 min) and cruise intervals (e.g. 3–5×8–10 min, short jog).
  Progress: Extend time-at-threshold week to week before raising pace.
• VO₂max [methodology]: Accumulate time near 95–100% VO₂max in repeats.
  Program: Reps 3–5 min (e.g. 5×3 min) or shorter (8–12×400–800 m); 1×/week in a VO₂ block.
  Progress: Add reps/total work before adding speed; cap the block at ~3–6 weeks.
• Running Economy [established]: Economy is a strong performance predictor independent of VO₂max.
  Program: Strides (4–8×~20 s), plyometrics, heavy/explosive strength, hill sprints.
  Progress: Introduce strides/plyos low-volume; build gradually; pair with base.
• Long Runs [accepted]: Duration matters more than pace for base; mostly Zone 2.
  Program: 1×/week; cap a single long run near ~30–35% of weekly volume.
  Progress: Lengthen gradually; insert a shorter "down" long run every 3–4 weeks.
• Race-Specific Programming [methodology]: Specificity rises as the event nears; general fitness is sharpened, not built.
  Program: Race-pace segments scaled to distance; rehearse fueling/logistics; taper volume ~40–60% over 1–2 wk (keep intensity).
  Progress: Increase race-pace continuity through the specific block, then taper.
• Recovery Science [established]: Sleep is the primary recovery driver; HRV/RHR trends reflect autonomic recovery.
  Program: Hard/easy sequencing; deload every 3–5 weeks; auto-regulate by readiness.
  Progress: Let recovery markers gate progression; ramp load smoothly (avoid acute spikes).

TRAINING BASELINES (from Week 1 assessment):
  Run: threshold=4:15/km · Z2 5:05–4:40/km · VO₂max intervals=3:50/km · VO₂max=58 ml/kg/min (Cooper Test)

Return ONLY a ```json ... ``` block. No prose outside it.

Each session object has an "id" (kebab-case), "name", "focus", and a "type" — one of:
  • "strength"  → include "exercises":[{ "name","sets":4,"reps":"8-10","load":"% 1RM / @RPE","rest":"2 min","cue":"…" }]
  • "endurance" → include "runType" (easy|tempo|threshold|intervals|long|recovery), "distance" (e.g. "10 km" or ""),
                  "duration" (e.g. "50 min"), "pace" (e.g. "4:45/km" or ""), "zone" (e.g. "Z2"), "intervals" (e.g. "6×800m @ 3:30, 90s jog" or ""), "rpe", "cue"
  • "fitstop"   → a class-style conditioning session: include "format" (e.g. "AMRAP 20 / 4 stations") and
                  "exercises":[{ "name","detail":"reps or time","cue":"…" }]

TOP-LEVEL schema:
{
  "name": "Descriptive program name",
  "type": "endurance",
  "weeks": 7,
  "sessionsPerWeek": 5,
  "progression": "One sentence on how the program evolves week to week",
  "sessions": [ /* the UNIQUE sessions used in a week (see shapes above) */ ],
  "dayMap": [ /* EXACTLY 7 entries for Mon,Tue,Wed,Thu,Fri,Sat,Sun — each is a session "id" or null for a rest day */ ],
  "weeklyProgressions": [
    { "week": 1, "note": "…" },
    { "week": 2, "setsAdd": 1, "note": "…" },
    { "week": 7, "deload": true, "note": "Deload — reduce volume ~50%" }
  ]
}

RULES:
• This is an ENDURANCE program. All sessions type "endurance". Build a balanced week: mostly easy/Zone-2 aerobic volume, with 1-2 quality sessions (tempo/threshold/intervals) and (if 5+ days) a long run. Provide pace OR zone targets and concrete interval structures. Respect easy/hard polarisation.
• dayMap: EXACTLY 7 entries (Mon→Sun). EXACTLY 5 of them are non-null session ids; the rest are null. Every non-null id MUST exist in "sessions". Distribute training days sensibly across the week.
• Define enough unique "sessions" to fill those 5 weekly slots (reuse an id on multiple days only if the session truly repeats).
• weeklyProgressions: EXACTLY 7 entries (weeks 1–6 working, week 7 has "deload": true). "setsAdd" (strength) cumulates from 0.
• Keep it realistic and specific. Every session needs concrete targets a trainee can execute and log.
prompt.user:
Generate the Endurance program.
Primary goal / weekly structure (user's words): Sub-3:30 marathon — high aerobic volume, weekly long run progressing to 32km, one threshold and one easy quality session, strict polarised easy/hard.
Total sessions/week: 5
Working weeks: 6 + 1 deload week (total 7)

program.name: Endurance Block
program.progression: Volume rises weekly, deload last week.
